summary: Aggregate decomp-matching cleanup across item code, item collision, fox blaster, and a few gm_1601 routines. The author framed it as high-fuzzy-match low-hanging fruit; the decomp.dev bot reported +0.08% matched code (+3040 bytes), 16 new 100% matches, and 7 additional improvements. Most edits tune types, locals, stack padding, branch shape, wrapper helpers, and temporary variables rather than adding new gameplay behavior.

key_files.role: Main item cleanup file; changed item selection helpers, collision response callbacks, wrapper helpers, stack padding, and mpCheckAllRemap call shape.
Header signature sync for it_266F changes, including int return/pointer types and declarations for new wrapper helpers.
Item collision cleanup; replaced dead/unrolled alternatives in hitbox-copy search with an ARRAY_SIZE loop and adjusted bounds flipping logic.
Small matching tweak in fox blaster setup with an explicitly commented likely fake improvement.
Small game-mode matching cleanup: include/types, local renames, branch-shape changes, buffer typing, and explicit bool compare.
Header signature update for fn_80169574 from void* buffer to s8* buffer.

what_changed: In it_266F, the item-selection helpers were cleaned up: it_8026C530 now uses clearer midpoint/index locals and adjacent u16 table checks, while it_8026C65C changed from returning u8 to int and uses a count local plus the revised table lookup pattern.

Several it_266F collision-response routines now use extra bool temporaries such as new_var/new_val, adjusted PAD_STACK sizes, and callback calls without redundant casts in places to improve matching while preserving the repeated collision-bitmask logic.

New wrapper helpers were added for selected it_266F calls: it_8026E15C_inline1, it_8026E15C_inline2, it_8026E248_inline, and it_8026E32C_inline. They wrap it_8026DDFC or it_8026DD5C and are declared in it_266F.h.
it_8026E7E0 was wrapped with #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop and had its chk/chk2 handling reshaped with a single new_var temporary; the bot reported this function reached 100%.
it_8026EA20 changed arg4 from UNK_T* to int*, passes arg4 directly to mpCheckAllRemap instead of *arg4, adds an UNUSED int _, and reduces PAD_STACK from 8 to 4; the header signature was updated.

itcoll.c removed large #if 0/unrolled alternatives in it_8026FCF8 and replaced them with a for loop over ARRAY_SIZE(item->x5D4_hitboxes), preserving the chk flag and early return. it_8026FC00 gained an empty item_gobj->next guard as a matching artifact.
it_80271A58 now obtains the item via GET_ITEM(item_gobj) and flips left/right bounds using a temp so the signed swap is explicit instead of independently negating both sides.

gm_1601.c added gm/types.h, retuned gm_80162A98 temporaries, renamed gm_80163298 locals to index/record/save_data/offset/score while retaining pointer math with a TODO, rewrote gm_80167470 as a switch on gm_801A4310 case 0x1F, changed fn_80169574 to take s8* buf, and made fn_8016A488 compare hud_enabled == true.
itfoxblaster.c added new_var2 in it_802ADF10 for two zero assignments and explicitly comments that this is probably a fake improvement because the idiomatic for-loop form was not yet matching.
smart_moves: Used small compiler-shape levers, not broad rewrites: temporary aliases, bool copies, adjusted stack padding, explicit casts, an empty guard, and #pragma dont_inline were enough to convert many high-fuzzy functions to matches.

Synchronized implementation and headers when types became clearer: fn_80169574 uses s8* so buf[size] = -2 no longer needs a cast, it_8026C65C returns int, and it_8026EA20 exposes int* for the remap argument.

Turned a noisy itcoll hitbox search with deleted #if attempts into a compact ARRAY_SIZE loop while still matching, showing that idiomatic loops can be recovered if the flag/break/return shape is preserved.
Left uncertainty visible instead of over-claiming: gm_80163298 keeps a TODO about pointer math being hard because of addi + lwzx, and itfoxblaster labels new_var2 as a likely fake improvement.
Validated the cleanup with automated match metrics: decomp.dev reported 16 new matches and 7 further improvements, including several item collision callbacks reaching 100%.
decomp_lessons: Header prototypes are part of the match: changing pointer signedness or scalar width in the .h and .c together can remove casts and alter codegen in the intended way.

For repeated item collision callbacks, preserving the compiler's chosen bool/register lifetime can require explicit temporary variables before bitmask tests such as & 0xF, & 0xE, & 0xD, & 0xC, and & 1.

Do not force unclear struct access when target code wants indexed pointer math; gm_80163298 documents that the save-data offset pattern still needs investigation because the addi + lwzx sequence is hard to express cleanly.
Wrapper helpers around tiny predicates can be useful matching tools, but they should remain searchable and clearly named after the parent routine until a better inline/static story is known.
A cleanup PR can combine low-hanging fuzzy matches across nearby systems, but comments should distinguish real readability wins from matching-only artifacts such as new_var2 or empty if blocks.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions keep address-based names such as it_8026E7E0, gm_80167470, and fn_80169574.
New helper names use the parent symbol plus an _inline suffix, with numeric suffixes when multiple wrappers exist: it_8026E15C_inline1 and it_8026E15C_inline2.
Headers preserve address comments beside prototypes, for example /* 26C65C */ and /* 169574 */.
Where semantics are somewhat known, locals were improved to names like index, record, save_data, offset, score, mid, and count.
Where a variable is purely for matching/codegen, the diff keeps generic names such as new_var, new_val, and new_var2 rather than inventing meaning.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Adjusted PAD_STACK values in several item functions, including it_8026E_inline, it_8026E32C, it_8026E7E0, and it_8026EA20.
Used #pragma dont_inline around it_8026E7E0 to control function codegen.
Introduced wrapper functions for it_8026DDFC and it_8026DD5C calls to affect call/inlining shape in it_8026E15C, it_8026E248, and it_8026E32C.
Introduced temporary bool/value variables before bitmask branches to preserve register lifetimes and branch ordering.
Changed branch spelling to match target shape, including gm_80167470's switch/case form and fn_8016A488's explicit hud_enabled == true.
Used typed pointer arithmetic such as temp_r9 = temp_r10 + 1 and temp_r9[mid] in it_8026C530 instead of raw integer offset expressions.
Kept a matching-only empty if (item_gobj->next) block in it_8026FC00.
Replaced hand-unrolled hitbox checks in it_8026FCF8 with a for loop over ARRAY_SIZE while preserving chk and early-return behavior.
review_feedback: Author PR body: this was intended as an aggregate PR for multiple files, ideally matching high-fuzzy low-hanging fruit in item files.
No human review comments or review bodies are present in the available slice.
decomp.dev bot report for GALE01: matched code reached 54.29%, up +0.08% and +3040 bytes.
Bot reported 16 new matches, including it_8026F6BC, it_8026E7E0, it_8026C65C, it_80271A58, it_8026FCF8, it_8026FC00, gm_80167470, it_8026C47C, fn_80169574, and fn_8016A488.
Bot reported additional improvements in it_8026E15C, it_8026E248, it_8026E32C, gm_801674C4, it_802ADF10, it_8026C530, and gm_80162A98.
follow_up_queries: gm_80163298 save_data offset pointer math addi lwzx struct field cleanup
